headline: Arm to Sell Its Own Chips, Eyeing Sales Goal of $15 Billion
summary: (Bloomberg) -- Arm Holdings Plc, which made its name licensing technology to semiconductor makers, will begin selling its own chips for the first time, adding a business that it expects to generate about $15 billion annually within five years. Meta Platforms Inc. will be the first major customer for the company’s chip, called an AGI CPU, Arm said Tuesday at an event in San Francisco. The product will have as many as 136 cores — a measure of processing power — and draw 300 watts of electricity, A

content.summary: The global PC market has clawed its way back from a brutal slump in 2022 and 2023, when shipments cratered amid post-pandemic oversupply, with 2024 delivering modest growth as businesses and consumers began refreshing aging fleets. By 2025, the rebound appeared to be accelerating sharply — shipments rose 9.3% year-over-year, fueled by commercial upgrades to ... RAMageddon Arrives: AI’s Endless Appetite Just Killed the PC Comeback

headline: These Stocks Are Today’s Movers: Arm, Intel, Meta, Alibaba, JD.com, Robinhood, GameStop, and More
summary: Stocks were rising Wednesday as investors held out hope for a swift end to the war in the Middle East amid cease-fire talks between the U.S. and Iran.  While shares initially fell Tuesday when the company unveiled its plans to enter the market for data-center chips, Arm laid out a series of aggressive sales targets for the coming years in after-hours trading, boosting shares.  Intel  and  Advanced Micro Devices  rose 6.9% and 6.6%, respectively.

headline: Arm shares rally as new AI chip to drive billions in annual revenue
summary: Arm sparked a rally in shares of companies that make central processors on Wednesday with a prediction that its new ‌data-center chip would bring in billions of dollars in annual revenue.  Shares of the ‌SoftBank Group-controlled company soared 20% to their highest since November, while rivals Intel and Advanced Micro Devices also advanced ​more than 5% each.  Arm expects the data-center chip to generate roughly $15 billion in annual revenue in about five years, CEO Rene Haas said in an interview with Reuters.

content.title: Arm shares rally as new AI chip to drive billions in annual revenue
content.summary: Arm sparked a rally in shares of companies that make central processors on Wednesday with a prediction that its new ‌data-center chip would bring in billions of dollars in annual revenue.  Shares of the ‌SoftBank Group-controlled company soared 20% to their highest since November, while rivals Intel and Advanced Micro Devices also advanced ​more than 5% each.  Arm expects the data-center chip to generate roughly $15 billion in annual revenue in about five years, CEO Rene Haas said in an interview with Reuters.

thumbnail.caption: An Arm Holdings AGI CPU chip is displayed at Fort Mason in San Francisco
